title: National Park Service (NPS) Appropriations: TenYear Trends
summary: The National Park Service (NPS) receives appropriations in annual Interior, Environment, and Related Agencies appropriations acts. Over the past decade (FY2017-FY2026), NPS received both regular (annual) appropriations and, in some years, supplemental appropriations to address damage from hurricanes and other natural disasters. NPS’s FY2026 regular appropriation of $3.267 billion was 11% higher than FY2017 in...

title: Here’s How Much Aid the United States Has Sent Ukraine
summary: Eleven charts illustrate the extraordinary level of support the United States has provided Ukraine in recent years—though there has been no significant U.S. aid legislation since 2024, and combined European aid tops U.S. contributions.

title: The Balogun Red Card Reversal Shows That the U.S. Plays By Its Own Rules
summary: After Trump called FIFA to review the red card that suspended Folarin Balogun, soccer’s governing body cleared the U.S. striker to play in his team’s next World Cup match. The reversal deepens a perception among allies that Washington honors the rules only when convenient—on the field and off it.

title: Restoring Obscenity Regulation in an Internet Age
summary: Introduction Americans born after the mid-1990s have lived their entire lives in a world awash with hardcore pornography. Never has so much pornography been so available to so many at so little cost and with so little effort. Our laws leave pornography effectively unregulated. Our technology—including especially smartphones—brings portable, private porn shops to everyone’s phone and mostly free of charge. Political...

title: Unsnarling the Red Tape
summary: Regulatory complexity is increasingly recognized as an impediment to innovation, institutional responsiveness, and long-run economic growth, with regulatory accumulation estimated to reduce the U.S. GDP growth rate by nearly a full percentage point annually.

title: Victor Davis Hanson: The Left’s "Moderate" Veneer Is Cracking—And 2028 Is Shaping Up To Be A Total Disaster
summary: Hoover Institution fellow Victor Davis Hanson says the radical left continues to nominate extreme candidates under the guise of moderation, a strategy that is rapidly failing as the American public wakes up to their bankrupt agenda.

title: Victor Davis Hanson: Is America's Political Polarization Becoming Dangerous?
summary: Hoover Institution fellow Victor Davis Hanson discusses whether America's growing divide between red and blue states is creating new risks for the country's future. Hanson explains why today's political polarization concerns him more than regional differences alone.

title: Our Freedom Still Needs Defending
summary: [Subscription Required] Social media and AI harm the body politic in ways Tocqueville and Lincoln couldn’t possibly foresee.

title: Victor Davis Hanson: Why Race Relations Are Getting Worse
summary: Hoover Institution fellow Victor Davis Hanson talks about the decline in America's race relations from the Chicago Juneteenth shooting, the Carmelo Anthony case, and the Caitlin Clark controversy. Hanson says America has lost much of the racial progress it made in the 1980s and 1990s.

title: Supreme Court lets Texas restrict minors' access to app stores for the time being
summary: Texas' App Store Accountability Act requires minors to have their parents' permission to download most apps. The Supreme Court says the law can go into effect as lawsuits continue in lower courts.

title: Graham Platner faces growing calls to withdraw following allegation of sexual assault
summary: The Democratic candidate for Senate in Maine said he was assessing next steps after allegations of sexual assault were reported on Monday by Politico. Platner denied them as "categorically untrue."

title: Man sues DHS after agents tracked him down for sending a scathing email to ICE
summary: Federal agents tried to track David Streever to his home and hotel, and left him a warning notice that a critical email he sent the former head of ICE may have been illegal. Now he's suing.

title: Under Trump, spouses of U.S. citizens face policy changes in the immigration system
summary: Spouses of U.S. citizens have traditionally had a special place in immigration law. That's no longer the case, according to the administration and immigration lawyers.

title: Democrats and Republicans pitch different visions of America on July 4th
summary: Both parties used the Fourth of July holiday to pitch their vision for the country.

title: U.S. World Cup star's suspension lifted after Trump's call to FIFA president
summary: The United States takes on Belgium Monday with star striker Folarin Balogun on the pitch. Balogun was given a red card in last week's game against Bosnia and Herzegovina, meaning he would miss the next game. But President Trump intervened and FIFA reversed Balogun's suspension. Amna Nawaz discussed the World Cup controversy with Liz Landers and Leander Schaerlaeckens, author of "The Long Game."

title: Trump administration takes major steps to roll back gun regulations
summary: During his race for the White House, President Trump pledged to expand Second Amendment rights nationwide. Now his administration is taking major steps to roll back gun restrictions put into place for public safety. Justice correspondent Ali Rogin discussed the administration's efforts with Chip Brownlee, a staff writer at The Trace, covering federal gun policy.

title: Tamara Keith and Carrie Dann on Platner facing calls to drop out of Maine Senate race
summary: NPR's Tamara Keith and Carrie Dann of the Cook Political Report join Amna Nawaz to discuss the latest political news, including Democrats in Maine scrambling and calling on Graham Platner to drop out of the Senate race as a sexual assault accuser comes forward, the direction of the Democratic Party into the midterms and what happened in Washington during the nation's 250th anniversary.

title: NATO chief demands allies present credible plans to reach defense spending targets
summary: NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte on Monday demanded that members put forward "clear, concrete and credible plans" to reach the organization's defense spending targets at its annual summit in Ankara.

title: A new ICE facility could speed up deportations for families and kids
summary: The Trump administration plans to open a 528-bed holding center for migrant families and unaccompanied children awaiting removal from the U.S., putting it next to a Louisiana airfield that has become the nation's largest hub of deportation flights.

title: Trump Expected to Tell Turkey He Is Ready to Restore Access to F-35 Jets
summary: The president, who is headed to a NATO summit in Ankara this week, had imposed the ban himself amid concerns that giving Turkey the jets could allow Russia to learn about their stealth technology.

title: Platner Faces Growing Democratic Calls to Drop Out
summary: Even supporters like Representative Ro Khanna of California called on the Democratic nominee to exit the Maine Senate race after a report that he had sexually assaulted a woman.

title: Trump Tries to Take Credit for Walmart Price Cuts of Beef and Other Products
summary: The retailer said it will lower prices for beef, produce, drinks, pools, toys, grills and summer clothing.

title: Who Might Replace Graham Platner if He Drops Out of the Maine Senate Race?
summary: Graham Platner can be replaced on the ballot if he withdraws in the next week. If he does, Maine Democrats would face an uncertain two-week race to choose a replacement.

title: What Polls Say About the Fight for the Senate
summary: Two of our political reporters dig into a revealing week of surveys.

title: Top Democrats pull Graham Platner endorsements, call on him to end Maine Senate campaign
summary: The Democrat, who has withstood other scandals since launching his challenge to Sen. Susan Collins (R), is accused of sexual assault by a woman he previously dated.

title: China test-fires long-range missile into Pacific, rattling U.S. allies
summary: The unprecedented test of a nuclear-capable submarine-launched missile highlights Beijing’s growing force in the region.

summary: As NATO leaders gather in Turkey, they are preparing for another summit shaped as much by managing President Donald Trump as by the alliance's security agenda.

summary: President Donald Trump confirms he asked Fifa to review United States striker Folarin Balogun's one-match suspension at the World Cup.

summary: President Zelensky says Sunday's "massive Russian attack" on Kyiv consisted of 68 missiles and 351 strike drones.

title: Charlie Kirk's family attends hearing for suspect charged with his murder
summary: Tyler Robinson, 23, is accused of murdering Kirk, a key Trump ally and founder of youth organisation Turning Point USA.

title: The Supreme Court, Mail-in Voting, Campaign Spending, and the 2026 Midterm Elections
summary: The Supreme Court’s decision this week on mail-in ballots got the most attention, but its decision on campaign spending may have a bigger impact on the fight for control of Congress.

title: A Year Later, Trump’s Big Beautiful Bill Act Has a Clean Tech Silver Lining
summary: Trump’s One Big Beautiful Bill Act gutted the wind and solar credits at the heart of Biden’s Inflation Reduction Act. But a year on, the law’s preserved incentives for geothermal, advanced nuclear, and grid-scale storage may do more to cut global emissions than the credits it eliminated.

title: Behind the curve: Britain’s defence investment plan in Trump’s world
summary: The new defence investment plan is weighed down by the habits of Britain’s defence establishment. By contrast, the British public appear readier to accept change.

title: Regional roots, global decisions: Andy Burnham’s foreign policy challenge
summary: Andy Burnham, Britain’s likely next prime minister, is clear-eyed about his domestic political priorities. But, with the world order in flux, all eyes are on his foreign policy approach

title: The future is fermented: How Europe can succeed in the next industrial race
summary: The next industrial revolution will be built with microbes as much as machines. China understands this. Europe still has the science to compete but must act quickly before industrial leadership slips away again

title: The China challenge
summary: Mark Leonard welcomes Mike Kuiken and Randy Schriver to examine the evolving US-China relationship and what it means for Europe

title: NATO: Issues for the July 2026 Ankara Summit
summary: Heads of state and government from the North Atlantic Treaty Organization’s (NATO’s) 32 members are scheduled to meet for a summit in Ankara, Turkey, on July 7-8, 2026. NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte has outlined three core priorities for the summit: continuing to increase allied defense investment, bolstering transatlantic defense industrial production, and supporting Ukraine. In Ankara, allied leaders also are...

title: Recent Litigation Highlights Debate About IRS Enforcement of Prohibition on Political Campaign Activity Against Churches
summary: Congress has granted federal income-tax exemptions to groups defined as religious, educational, or public-interest organizations under Section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code, but restricts those groups (known as “Section 501(c)(3) organizations”) from participating in political campaign activity as a condition to receiving an exemption. On March 31, 2026, the U.S. District Court for the Eastern District of...

title: Food Additives and GRAS Substances: A Legal Framework
summary: Over the past few decades, the Food and Drug Administration’s (FDA’s) regulation of food additives has provoked debate. In general, food additives, which include substances such as spices, emulsifiers, and preservatives, are added to foods to improve taste, texture, shelf life, or for other reasons. title: Washington's Growing Portfolio: Tracking U.S. Government Investments
summary: CFR’s U.S. Government Deal Tracker shows how the government is experimenting with new tools and financing structures to advance a range of strategic sectors, including critical minerals, energy, global logistics, manufacturing, telecommunications, and other technologies.

title: Unconventional deterrence: How Europe can ward off a Russian attack
summary: A nuclear deterrent does not preclude major war. Putin has learnt this lesson—now Europe needs to do the same.

title: Insights from table-top exercises in Europe on AI safety and cyber misuse
summary: This report presents findings from table-top exercises with senior policymakers in Germany, the Netherlands, and France, identifying governance gaps and capability priorities for responding to AI-enabled cyberattack crises.

title: How Schools and After-School Programs Can Collaborate to Support Academic Alignment
summary: The authors provide national data about the state of academic alignment between school-day and after-school learning, illustrate how schools and after-school programs put academic alignment into practice, and identify enabling and impeding factors.

title: Military Effectiveness Should Be Determined by The War Department Not Judges
summary: The military isn't a therapeutic institution; its purpose is to deter war, fight war, and win war. There are hard truths in military life that a humane society may dislike but cannot repeal. The first is that military service is not an entitlement. It is a calling, yes; an honor, certainly. But it is also a profession whose members must be prepared to deploy, fight, endure privation, and, if necessary, die in...

title: America’s Asian Allies Want Nuclear Submarines. Here’s How To Build Them
summary: Nuclear power is cool once again and is all the rage in Asia. The North Koreans are getting in on the action with the rumored development of a nuclear-powered submarine. title: Supply Chain, Energy, and AI Nexus
summary: The authors identify the inventory of critical electrical equipment for artificial intelligence (AI) data center operations and the impact of supply chain vulnerabilities on the U.S. power grid’s ability to meet AI demand by 2030.

title: Can LLM Agents Select and Engage with Biological Tools?
summary: Large language model agents are capable of performing initial interactions with biological tools (BTs), indicating that agents could lower expertise barriers for actors seeking to use BTs. Targeted testing of agent design capabilities is warranted.

title: AI agents put offensive cyber within reach of novices
summary: Agentic AI models now enable non-experts to conduct advanced offensive cyberattacks. This study finds AI agents can autonomously breach complex systems rapidly, highlighting urgent needs for evolved defence and better cyber risk assessments.
